channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Actor Bill Hader 'in shock' returning to Palisades Fire neighborhood
item.description: "Barry" actor Bill Hader's Pacific Palisades home survived the catastrophic wildfire, but he wasn't prepared when he witnessed the devastation surrounding the area upon his return. "People keep saying 'unreal,' it does. It doesn't seem real," he explained to KTLA 5. "I'm sorry I'm in shock.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, NewsNation

item.title: New wildfire warnings issued and more power is shut off as winds rise in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Millions Southern Californians faced new wildfire warnings Tuesday, and tens of thousands saw their power shut off as strong winds blew across the parched landscape around Los Angeles where two massive blazes have been burning for a week.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House barrels toward fight over placing conditions on California fire aid
item.description: The House is barreling toward a contentious debate over disaster relief for California as many — but not all — Republicans call for conditions to be placed on the assistance, an idea Democrats are soundly rejecting. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean law enforcement officials enter presidential compound to detain impeached Yoon
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Hundreds of law enforcement officials in South Korea entered the residential compound of impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol on Wednesday morning in the capital Seoul. It was their second attempt to detain him over his imposition of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Border crossings in December trend low as Biden administration wraps up
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. officials on Tuesday announced the latest border data, and it suggests the Biden administration is poised to end its term without an expected bump in illegal border crossings.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: USDA documented insects and slime at Boar's Head plants, records show
item.description: Government inspectors documented unsanitary conditions at several Boar's Head deli meat plants, not just the factory that was shut down last year after a deadly outbreak of listeria poisoning, federal records show.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: New Orleans Police can begin the process of ending federal oversight
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The New Orleans Police Department can begin ending its longstanding federal oversight, a judge ruled Tuesday in response to a request from the city and the Justice Department to wind down monitoring.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Man pleads guilty in string of MS-13 killings that stunned New York suburbs
item.description: CENTRAL ISLIP, N.Y. (AP) — A man who helped lead an MS-13 clique in New York pleaded guilty Tuesday in a federal racketeering case involving seven murders, including the 2016 killings of two high school girls that focused the nation’s attention on the violent Central American street gang.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prominent Puerto Rican civil rights leader José 'Cha Cha' Jiménez dies at 76
item.description: José “Cha Cha” Jiménez, a prominent civil rights and liberation movement figure and founder of the Young Lords in Chicago and Co-Founder of the Rainbow Coalition, has died.
item.dc:creator: Fernanda Figueroa, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden moves to lift state sponsor of terrorism designation for Cuba, part of deal to free prisoners
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden notified Congress of his intent to lift the U.S. designation of Cuba as a state sponsor of terrorism, the White House announced, as part of a deal facilitated by the Catholic Church to free political prisoners on the island. Senior U.S.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, COLLEEN LONG, MATTHEW LEE and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Interactive maps show homes damaged, destroyed in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: The Los Angeles County Fire Department has provided maps of the thousands of structures, primarily homes, that were damaged or destroyed in the Palisades and Eaton wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hegseth survives attacks from Democrats in testy Senate hearing
item.description: Pete Hegseth on Tuesday battled through a high-stakes Senate hearing in which Democrats sought to paint the Pentagon nominee as inexperienced, unprofessional and toxic due to allegations of sexual abuse and heavy drinking.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5

item.title: Capital One sued by US watchdog, accused of cheating customers out of $2 billion
item.description: A U.S. watchdog is suing Capital One for allegedly misleading consumers about its offerings for high-interest savings accounts — and “cheating" customers out of more than $2 billion in lost interest payments as a result.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chemicals in sewage sludge fertilizer pose cancer risk, EPA says
item.description: Harmful chemicals in sewage sludge that is spread on pasture land as fertilizer are causing cancer, the Environmental Protection Agency said Tuesday. The risk is highest for people who regularly consume milk, beef and other products from farms where it is spread.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A retro blue VW van miraculously survives deadly Los Angeles fire
item.description: A surprising AP photograph of an undamaged Volkswagen van surrounded by destruction from a Los Angeles wildfire has astonished viewers.
item.dc:creator: Janie Har, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NYC looks to cut emissions from charboilers at restaurants
item.description: Under the proposed rule, restaurants with charbroil grills would need to install an emissions control device.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bass, NewsNation

item.title: Montréal Canadiens hockey player struck by car while in Salt Lake City
item.description: The team later confirmed Emil Heineman suffered an "upper-body" injury and is expected to be sidelined for three to four weeks.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, NewsNation

item.title: Remains found in backyard grave, man arrested for cold case murder
item.description: Murphy Henry, 54, was also charged with abuse of a corpse and tampering with evidence, related to the disappearance of Staggs.
item.dc:creator: Matt Rawlings, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth grilled over comments on women serving in combat
item.description: The start to hearings for President-elect Donald Trump’s cabinet nominees kicked off on Tuesday with Pete Hegseth who is hoping to become the next secretary of defense.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WGN 9

item.title: Blinken makes the case for post-war reconstruction, security and governance of Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken made a last-minute case Tuesday for a plan for the post-war reconstruction and governance of Gaza as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas appears tantalizingly close to completion.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Contaminated drinking water is a growing concern for cities facing wildfires
item.description: As fires continue to burn across Los Angeles, several utilities have declared their drinking water unsafe until extensive testing can prove otherwise. A warmer, drier climate means wildfires are getting worse, and encroaching on cities — with devastating impact.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge approves emergency order to close migrant gang-infested Aurora, Colorado, apartment complex
item.description: An Aurora, Colorado, judge ordered the closure of the Edge at Lowry apartments on Tuesday, a complex at the center of violent gang crime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: READ: Special counsel's final report in Trump Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department released special counsel Jack Smith's report on President-elect Donald Trump's election interference case, just days before he returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Poll reveals how much voters blame climate change, Newsom for California fire crisis
item.description: In the wake of several deadly California wildfires a new poll shows how Americans are reacting to the disaster, which left at least two dozen people dead and reduced entire neighborhoods to charred rubble.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Live updates: Democrats turn up heat on Hegseth as Republican alleges hypocrisy
item.description: Defense Secretary nominee Pete Hegseth appeared before the Senate Armed Services Committee on Tuesday, the first of President-elect Trump's Cabinet candidates to face confirmation.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: Maquiladora sites to help Mexican border town shelter deported migrants
item.description: The Mexican town of Piedras Negras, across from Eagle Pass, Texas, is readying plans to open warehouses formerly used as maquiladoras, if needed, to house and help deported migrants if the incoming Trump administration sends masses south of the border.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Realtor-turned-murder suspect charged with client couple's slaying held at gunpoint by their son: police
item.description: North Carolina authorities have arrested David Lever, a Greenville-based real-estate agent, while investigating a triple homicide and four different crime scenes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Multiracial boom in 2020 census was mostly an illusion, researchers say
item.description: When the 2020 census results were released, they showed a boom in the number of people classified as multiracial in the United States since 2010. Two Princeton sociologists now say that jump was mostly an illusion.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sheriff calls on wildfire evacuees to be patient amid 'grim' search for bodies
item.description: Los Angeles County Sheriff Robert Luna asked community members to be patient with local officials, as residents eagerly await permission to return to their homes in southern California, where tens of thousands of acres have been decimated by wildfires in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Los Angeles price gouging is 'inhumane': Real estate agent
item.description: Nikko Santo Pietro, Los Angeles real estate agent and son of 'Wheel of Fortune’s' Vanna White, said the supply of housing is “so limited.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, KRON4

item.title: Colorado apartment complex where armed gang members were seen on video will be closed
item.description: AURORA, Colo. (AP) — A Colorado apartment complex where armed members of a Venezuelan gang were caught on video entering a unit last summer is expected to close in about a month under an emergency court order, city officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to save TikTok videos before the potential ban goes into effect
item.description: The deadline for a potential TikTok ban in the U.S. is fast approaching and many of the app's users are already taking steps to prepare, even as its fate remains uncertain.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Carrie Underwood to perform at Trump inauguration
item.description: Country music star Carrie Underwood is set to take the stage on Inauguration Day, a sign that President-elect Trump's second inauguration may be drawing more high-profile talent than his first go round.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Oscars push back nominations announcement amid Calif. wildfires
item.description: With fires still active in the Los Angeles area, the film academy also extended the nominations voting period for its members through Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: List: The 'overused' words banished for 2025 by a Michigan university
item.description: Sorry not sorry, but one Michigan university would appreciate if you did not utilize these words, period.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, ABC 4

item.title: Former Disney World employee to plead guilty to hacking menus, changing allergen information: court records
item.description: A former Walt Disney World employee will plead guilty to hacking menu-creation software to include profanity and alter allergy information, court records indicate.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KRON4

item.title: Santa Anita postpones weekend racing as track is staging area for fire relief efforts
item.description: A flareup of the wildfire on the west side of Los Angeles that prompted new evacuations has caused Santa Anita to cancel horse racing this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5
